newDirectories.name: CDNs
newDirectories.description: Content delivery networks that cache and serve assets close to users.
newDirectories.heroCopy: Edge latency, cache control, cost, DX, ecosystem reach — see where each network wins.

aspects.label: Latency
aspects.description: Edge proximity and how fast cached assets reach users.

aspects.label: Cache control
aspects.description: Granularity of purging, TTLs, and edge cache rules.

aspects.label: Cost
aspects.description: Value relative to price, including egress (5 = great deal).

aspects.label: Developer experience
aspects.description: APIs, dashboards, docs, and day-to-day ergonomics.

aspects.label: Ecosystem
aspects.description: Integrations, edge compute, and surrounding platform breadth.

items.name: Cloudflare CDN
items.description: Global CDN spanning 300+ cities with a generous free tier and integrated security and edge compute.

items.name: Fastly
items.description: Developer-focused edge cloud with instant purging and programmable VCL/Compute edge logic.

items.name: Akamai
items.description: The largest enterprise CDN, powering streaming, gaming, and finance at massive scale.

items.name: Amazon CloudFront
items.description: AWS-native CDN tightly integrated with S3, Lambda@Edge, and the rest of AWS.

items.name: Bunny.net
items.description: Budget-friendly CDN with pay-as-you-go pricing, built-in optimizer, and edge storage.

items.name: Gcore CDN
items.description: Global CDN with 180+ points of presence and low-latency edge delivery for media and apps.

items.name: KeyCDN
items.description: Simple pay-as-you-go HTTP/2 CDN with 50+ global POPs aimed at small teams and sites.

items.name: CDN77
items.description: Performance-focused CDN with 50+ data centers, strong for VOD and live video delivery.

items.name: Azure Front Door / CDN
items.description: Microsoft's global CDN and edge delivery layer integrated with Azure services.

items.name: Google Cloud CDN
items.description: GCP-native CDN serving content from Google's global edge with low latency and high throughput.

items.name: jsDelivr
items.description: Free public CDN for open-source libraries, npm packages, GitHub repos, and static assets.

items.name: Vercel Edge Network
items.description: Global edge CDN baked into Vercel deployments, optimized for Next.js and frontend apps.

items.name: Netlify CDN
items.description: Git-driven CDN for static frontends and Jamstack sites with atomic deploys and edge functions.

items.name: StackPath
items.description: Edge platform offering CDN, WAF, and edge compute across roughly 60 data centers.

newDirectories.name: Managed DNS
newDirectories.description: Authoritative DNS hosting and management providers.
newDirectories.heroCopy: Resolution latency, reliability, cost, DX, ecosystem — pick the nameservers your stack depends on.

aspects.label: Latency
aspects.description: Resolution speed from Anycast networks worldwide.

aspects.label: Reliability
aspects.description: Uptime, SLAs, and resilience against outages and attacks.

aspects.label: Cost
aspects.description: Value relative to price including query-based fees (5 = great deal).

aspects.label: Developer experience
aspects.description: APIs, terraform support, dashboards, and ergonomics.

aspects.label: Ecosystem
aspects.description: Integrations, automation, and surrounding platform breadth.

items.name: Cloudflare DNS
items.description: Top-ranked managed DNS with a free tier, unlimited queries, and a fast global Anycast network.

items.name: Amazon Route 53
items.description: AWS's scalable DNS with health checks, latency-based routing, and pay-as-you-go pricing.

items.name: Google Cloud DNS
items.description: GCP-native authoritative DNS with 100% uptime SLA and pay-per-query pricing.

items.name: Azure DNS
items.description: Microsoft's managed DNS with Alias records that dynamically reference Azure resources.

items.name: NS1 Connect (IBM)
items.description: Enterprise DNS with advanced traffic steering, filter chains, and data-driven routing.

items.name: DNSimple
items.description: Developer-friendly managed DNS and domain registrar with a clean API and easy record editing.

items.name: DNS Made Easy
items.description: Long-running business DNS provider known for fast resolution and strong uptime guarantees.

items.name: Oracle Dyn DNS
items.description: Enterprise managed DNS (formerly Dyn) with traffic steering inside Oracle Cloud Infrastructure.

items.name: Akamai Edge DNS
items.description: Carrier-grade authoritative DNS on Akamai's massive Anycast network with built-in DDoS resilience.

items.name: Namecheap FreeDNS
items.description: Free and premium DNS hosting bundled with Namecheap's popular domain registrar.

items.name: Bunny DNS
items.description: Low-latency Anycast DNS from Bunny.net with smart geo and latency-based routing.

items.name: deSEC
items.description: Free, security-focused DNS hosting built on open-source software with DNSSEC by default.

items.name: Hurricane Electric DNS
items.description: Free authoritative DNS hosting from the global IPv6-friendly network operator.

newDirectories.name: Secrets management
newDirectories.description: Platforms for storing, rotating, and distributing secrets and credentials.
newDirectories.heroCopy: Security depth, integrations, cost, DX, ecosystem — choose where your keys live.

aspects.label: Security
aspects.description: Encryption model, dynamic secrets, rotation, and audit depth.

aspects.label: Integrations
aspects.description: Breadth of cloud, CI/CD, Kubernetes, and SDK connectors.

aspects.label: Cost
aspects.description: Value relative to price and licensing (5 = great deal).

aspects.label: Developer experience
aspects.description: CLI, SDKs, dashboards, and day-to-day ergonomics.

aspects.label: Ecosystem
aspects.description: Community, tooling, and surrounding platform breadth.

items.name: HashiCorp Vault
items.description: The most mature secrets platform with full dynamic secrets and multi-cloud credential brokering.

items.name: AWS Secrets Manager
items.description: AWS-native secrets store with automatic rotation and tight IAM integration.

items.name: Azure Key Vault
items.description: Microsoft's managed vault for secrets, keys, and certificates with HSM-backed options.

items.name: Google Secret Manager
items.description: GCP-native secret store with versioning, IAM bindings, and rotation notifications.

items.name: Doppler
items.description: Developer-first SaaS secrets manager with strong CLI, sync integrations, and access controls.

items.name: Infisical
items.description: Open-source secrets, certificates, and access platform with a Kubernetes operator and CSI driver.

items.name: Akeyless
items.description: SaaS secrets platform using Distributed Fragments Cryptography so no single party holds a full key.

items.name: CyberArk Conjur
items.description: Enterprise secrets management with policy-as-code and deep PAM integration for DevOps workloads.

items.name: OpenBao

items.name: 1Password Secrets Automation

items.name: Bitwarden Secrets Manager
items.description: Open-source secrets manager extending Bitwarden with CLI, SDKs, and machine accounts.

items.name: Keeper Secrets Manager
items.description: FedRAMP-authorized, zero-knowledge secrets manager aimed at regulated and enterprise teams.

items.name: SOPS
items.description: CLI tool that encrypts secrets in-place inside version-controlled YAML, JSON, and ENV files.

newDirectories.name: Message brokers

newDirectories.heroCopy: Throughput, durability, cost, DX, ecosystem — route your events through the right pipe.

aspects.label: Throughput
aspects.description: Sustained message rate and scalability under load.

aspects.label: Durability
aspects.description: Persistence, replication, and delivery guarantees.

aspects.label: Cost
aspects.description: Value relative to price and operational overhead (5 = great deal).

aspects.label: Developer experience
aspects.description: Client libraries, tooling, and day-to-day ergonomics.

aspects.label: Ecosystem
aspects.description: Connectors, integrations, and surrounding platform breadth.

items.name: Apache Pulsar
items.description: Multi-tenant streaming platform with separated serving and storage layers and native geo-replication.

items.name: Amazon SQS
items.description: Fully-managed, elastic message queue for decoupling serverless and microservice workloads.

items.name: Azure Service Bus
items.description: Enterprise managed message broker with queues, topics, and sessions, popular in .NET stacks.

items.name: Confluent Cloud
items.description: Fully-managed Kafka from Kafka's creators with connectors, ksqlDB, and Stream Governance.

items.name: Apache ActiveMQ
items.description: Mature open-source JMS message broker supporting many protocols and classic enterprise messaging.

items.name: Solace PubSub+
items.description: Event-mesh broker with wire-speed routing across hybrid and multi-cloud and multi-protocol support.

items.name: Redis Streams
items.description: Append-only log data type in Redis providing lightweight, low-latency streaming and consumer groups.

items.name: Eclipse Mosquitto
items.description: Lightweight open-source MQTT broker for IoT telemetry across millions of intermittent devices.

items.name: ZeroMQ
items.description: Brokerless messaging library extending sockets with async queues and multiple messaging patterns.

newDirectories.name: Realtime collaboration
newDirectories.description: Sync, presence, and multiplayer infrastructure for live, collaborative apps.
newDirectories.heroCopy: Latency, presence, scale, cost, and DX decide whether multiplayer feels instant or laggy.

aspects.label: Latency
aspects.description: End-to-end propagation speed of updates between clients.

aspects.label: Presence
aspects.description: Quality of presence, cursors, and conflict-free shared state primitives.

aspects.label: Cost
aspects.description: Pricing fit relative to connections, messages, and concurrency (5 = great deal).

aspects.label: Developer experience
aspects.description: SDKs, hooks, local dev, and debugging ergonomics.

aspects.label: Ecosystem
aspects.description: Framework integrations, examples, and community depth.

items.name: Liveblocks
items.description: Managed collaboration platform with rooms, presence, cursors, and conflict-free data structures.

items.name: Pusher Channels
items.description: Hosted pub/sub WebSocket service for live updates, presence, and client events.

items.name: Ably
items.description: Global realtime messaging platform with pub/sub, presence, and guaranteed message delivery.

items.name: PartyKit
items.description: Realtime multiplayer infrastructure running stateful logic on Cloudflare's edge.

items.name: Supabase Realtime
items.description: Postgres change-data-capture, broadcast, and presence channels with row-level security.

items.name: Yjs
items.description: Open-source CRDT framework for building conflict-free collaborative editing and shared state.

items.name: Hocuspocus
items.description: Yjs-native WebSocket backend purpose-built for collaborative document editing.

items.name: Tiptap Collaboration
items.description: Managed Yjs collaboration backend for the Tiptap rich-text editor with cursors and comments.

items.name: LiveKit
items.description: Open-source WebRTC infrastructure for realtime audio, video, and data rooms.

items.name: Socket.IO
items.description: Self-hosted JavaScript library for bidirectional event-based realtime communication.

items.name: Firebase Realtime Database
items.description: Google's hosted NoSQL database that syncs JSON state across clients in realtime.

items.name: Centrifugo
items.description: Open-source scalable realtime messaging server for pub/sub, presence, and channels.

items.name: Cloudflare Realtime
items.description: Cloudflare's edge WebSocket and SFU infrastructure for serverless realtime apps.

items.name: Automerge
items.description: Open-source CRDT library for building local-first collaborative apps with automatic merging.

newDirectories.name: Notifications
newDirectories.description: Push, SMS, email, and in-app notification delivery and orchestration platforms.
newDirectories.heroCopy: Channel coverage, deliverability, cost, DX, and ecosystem shape whether messages land.

aspects.label: Channel coverage
aspects.description: Breadth of push, SMS, email, in-app, and chat channels supported.

aspects.label: Deliverability
aspects.description: Reliability, routing, fallbacks, and inbox/device delivery rates.

aspects.label: Cost
aspects.description: Pricing fit across subscribers, messages, and channels (5 = great deal).

aspects.label: Developer experience
aspects.description: APIs, SDKs, templating, local testing, and debugging quality.

aspects.label: Ecosystem
aspects.description: Provider integrations, framework support, and community depth.

items.name: Knock
items.description: Notification infrastructure that orchestrates product messaging across email, push, SMS, and chat.

items.name: Novu
items.description: Open-source notification infrastructure with workflows, a unified API, and an inbox component.

items.name: OneSignal
items.description: Push-first messaging platform delivering web, mobile push, email, SMS, and in-app at scale.

items.name: Twilio
items.description: Programmable communications APIs for SMS, voice, WhatsApp, and verification.

items.name: Firebase Cloud Messaging
items.description: Google's free cross-platform push notification delivery for web, Android, and iOS.

items.name: Pusher Beams
items.description: Hosted push notification API for web and mobile with device interest targeting.

items.name: SuprSend
items.description: Notification infrastructure with multi-channel routing, batching, and built-in observability.

items.name: Fyno
items.description: Notification orchestration layer that routes across many third-party providers from one API.

items.name: MagicBell
items.description: Notification inbox and multi-channel delivery API with prebuilt in-app UI components.

items.name: Vonage
items.description: Communications APIs for SMS, voice, messaging, and verification across global carriers.

items.name: Bird (MessageBird)
items.description: Omnichannel CPaaS for SMS, WhatsApp, email, and voice with marketing and support flows.

items.name: Expo Push Notifications
items.description: Free push notification service for React Native and Expo apps via a single API.

items.name: Engagespot
items.description: Notification API and inbox widget for multi-channel product notifications.

newDirectories.name: Data warehouses
newDirectories.description: Analytical and OLAP stores for large-scale querying, BI, and real-time analytics.
newDirectories.heroCopy: Query speed, scalability, cost, DX, and ecosystem decide how fast insight arrives.

aspects.label: Query speed
aspects.description: Latency on analytical scans, aggregations, and concurrent queries.

aspects.label: Scalability
aspects.description: Headroom for data volume, concurrency, and storage/compute separation.

aspects.label: Cost
aspects.description: Pricing predictability at real query and storage volume (5 = great deal).

aspects.label: Developer experience
aspects.description: SQL ergonomics, tooling, setup, and operational simplicity.

aspects.label: Ecosystem
aspects.description: Connectors, BI tools, ETL, and open-format compatibility.

items.name: Snowflake
items.description: Cloud data platform with separated storage and compute and mature governance features.

items.name: Google BigQuery
items.description: Serverless, zero-ops analytical warehouse with separated storage and on-demand compute.

items.name: Databricks
items.description: Lakehouse platform unifying SQL analytics and ML on open table formats.

items.name: Amazon Redshift
items.description: AWS-native columnar data warehouse for large, predictable analytical workloads.

items.name: MotherDuck
items.description: Serverless cloud analytics platform built on DuckDB with fast spin-up for customer-facing analytics.

items.name: Firebolt
items.description: Cloud data warehouse engineered for low-latency, high-concurrency analytics at scale.

items.name: Apache Druid
items.description: Open-source real-time analytics database for sub-second OLAP on event streams.

items.name: Apache Pinot
items.description: Open-source distributed OLAP datastore for ultra-low-latency user-facing analytics.

items.name: Dremio
items.description: Lakehouse query engine for fast SQL directly on data lakes and open table formats.

items.name: StarRocks
items.description: Open-source MPP analytical database for high-concurrency real-time OLAP queries.

items.name: Azure Synapse Analytics
items.description: Microsoft's integrated analytics service combining data warehousing and big data.

newDirectories.name: Uptime monitoring
newDirectories.description: Uptime, status-page, and synthetic monitoring for catching outages before users do.
newDirectories.heroCopy: Check frequency, alerting, cost, DX, and ecosystem keep ops calm.

aspects.label: Check frequency
aspects.description: Polling interval, global probe locations, and check type breadth.

aspects.label: Alerting
aspects.description: Notification channels, on-call routing, escalation, and noise control.

aspects.label: Cost
aspects.description: Pricing fit across monitors, seats, and check frequency (5 = great deal).

aspects.label: Developer experience
aspects.description: Setup, status-page quality, dashboards, and monitoring-as-code support.

aspects.label: Ecosystem
aspects.description: Alert destinations, incident tools, and integration connectors.

items.name: Better Stack Uptime
items.description: Uptime monitoring with incident management, on-call scheduling, and status pages in one platform.

items.name: Checkly
items.description: Code-first synthetic monitoring with Playwright browser checks and monitoring-as-code.

items.name: Pingdom
items.description: SolarWinds uptime, synthetic, and real-user monitoring from global probe locations.

items.name: UptimeRobot
items.description: Focused uptime monitoring with fast downtime alerts and simple status pages.

items.name: Uptime Kuma
items.description: Open-source self-hosted uptime monitoring tool with status pages and many notification channels.

items.name: StatusCake
items.description: Website uptime, page speed, domain, and SSL monitoring with public status pages.

items.name: Site24x7
items.description: Zoho's all-in-one monitoring suite covering uptime, synthetics, APM, and infrastructure.

items.name: Uptrends
items.description: Uptime, transaction, and real-user monitoring from a large network of global checkpoints.

items.name: Hyperping
items.description: Uptime and synthetic monitoring with polished status pages across global regions.

items.name: OneUptime
items.description: Open-source monitoring and observability platform with uptime checks, status pages, and incidents.

items.name: HetrixTools
items.description: Uptime, server, and blacklist monitoring with status pages and a generous free tier.

items.name: Datadog Synthetic Monitoring
items.description: API and browser synthetic tests integrated into Datadog's observability platform.

items.name: OnlineOrNot
items.description: Uptime and synthetic monitoring with cron job checks and status pages for small teams.

items.name: Cronitor
items.description: Uptime, cron job, and heartbeat monitoring with alerting and status pages.

newDirectories.name: Workflow automation
newDirectories.description: iPaaS and no-code/low-code platforms for connecting apps and automating workflows.
newDirectories.heroCopy: Integrations, ease of use, cost, DX, and ecosystem decide how much glue work disappears.

aspects.label: Integrations
aspects.description: Breadth of prebuilt app connectors and triggers/actions.

aspects.label: Ease of use
aspects.description: How approachable workflow building is for non-developers.

aspects.label: Cost
aspects.description: Pricing fit across tasks, operations, and seats (5 = great deal).

aspects.label: Developer experience
aspects.description: Code steps, APIs, version control, and extensibility for developers.

aspects.label: Ecosystem
aspects.description: Templates, community workflows, and integration marketplace depth.

items.name: Zapier
items.description: No-code automation connecting thousands of apps with triggers, actions, and AI steps.

items.name: Make
items.description: Visual no-code automation platform (formerly Integromat) with flexible scenario building.

items.name: Pipedream
items.description: Developer-first integration platform with code steps, prebuilt actions, and hosted runtime.

items.name: Workato
items.description: Enterprise iPaaS with governance, complex transformations, and prebuilt automation recipes.

items.name: Tray.ai
items.description: Developer-friendly enterprise iPaaS for building integrations and AI-powered automation.

items.name: Activepieces
items.description: Open-source no-code automation with a TypeScript pieces framework and self-hosting.

items.name: Node-RED
items.description: Open-source flow-based programming tool for wiring together APIs, devices, and services.

items.name: Automatisch
items.description: Open-source Zapier alternative for self-hosted business workflow automation.

items.name: IFTTT
items.description: Consumer-focused automation connecting apps and smart devices with simple applets.

items.name: MuleSoft
items.description: Salesforce's enterprise integration platform for API-led connectivity and complex iPaaS.

items.name: Boomi
items.description: Cloud-native iPaaS for integration, API management, and data workflows across enterprises.

items.name: Kestra
items.description: Open-source event-driven orchestration platform with declarative YAML workflows.
